channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Democrat Sherrill responds to DOJ election monitors in New Jersey governor's race
item.description: Mikie Sherrill said she will "ensure that we don't have any voter intimidation" in response to the DOJ sending election watchers to New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump travels to Asia and a meeting with China's Xi as the US government shutdown drags on
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE (AP) — President Donald Trump headed for Asia for the first time this term, a trip where he’s expected to work on investment deals and peace efforts before meeting face-to-face with Chinese President Xi Jinping to try to de-escalate a trade war.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARK SCHIEFELBEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tears down East Wing for $300M ballroom ahead of high-stakes China meeting
item.description: President Donald Trump knocks down the East Wing to build a $300 million ballroom and heads overseas for tense China talks that could reshape U.S. ties and global power dynamics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Potential 2028 contender campaigns for Spanberger in key Tidewater area
item.description: Pennsylvania governor Josh Shapiro campaigns for Virginia's Abigail Spanberger as 2028 presidential speculation grows around the Democratic rising star.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A report claims left-wing terrorism is rising. The data paints a complicated picture
item.description: The report's claim comes with caveats. Its critics say it does more to reveal issues around collecting and analyzing domestic terrorism data than it does to clarify the current state of the problem.
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef, MPR News

item.title: DHS to soon deport Abrego Garcia to African nation after illegal alien's return from El Salvador, filing says
item.description: The Trump administration says it could soon deport Salvadoran illegal alien and alleged MS-13 member Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia to Liberia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shutdowns began as a way to enforce federal law. Now Trump is using it to take more power
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The government shutdown, already the second-longest in history, with no end in sight, is quickly becoming a way for President Donald Trump to exercise new command over the government. It wasn't always this way.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AG James' 'horrible judgment' backing Mamdani puts New Yorkers in 'jeopardy': GOP challenger
item.description: GOP challenger Michael Henry slammed NY attorney general Letitia James over her endorsement of socialist mayoral candidate Zohran Mamdani in a Fox News Digital interview.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Virginia Giuffre's memoir recounts rape by former prime minister; Epstein's ties to Bill Clinton, Trump
item.description: Epstein accuser Virginia Giuffre's new memoir recounts years of abuse under the disgraced financier and the powerful figures around him, including an ex-prime minister who brutally raped her.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Over 1,000 high school students across San Diego County walk out for environment
item.description: More than 1,000 students across 19 high schools in San Diego County participated in a state-wide walkout on Friday, urging lawmakers to pass climate related legislation.
item.dc:creator: Chris Ponce, Fox 5

item.title: Trump ends trade talks with Canada over tariffs ad that Ontario premier now says he'll phase out
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced he’s ending “all trade negotiations” with Canada because of a television ad sponsored by one of its provinces that used the words of former President Ronald Reagan to criticize U.S. tariffs — prompting the province's leader to later pull the ad.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, SEUNG MIN KIM and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Reagan Foundation becomes the latest US institution drawn into Donald Trump's controversies
item.description: The Ronald Reagan Presidential Foundation and Institute is drawing a volatile mix of blowback and praise for arguing that a Canadian government advertisement playing in U.S. markets misrepresented the 40th president’s words to blast President Donald Trump’s tariff policies.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and THALIA BEATY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In emotional speech, Zohran Mamdani defends Muslim identity against 'racist and baseless' attacks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Zohran Mamdani, the Democratic nominee for New York City mayor, pledged Friday to further embrace his Muslim identity in response to growing attacks by former Gov. Andrew Cuomo and his surrogates that he characterized as “racist and baseless.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to jet off to Asia as North Korea fires ballistic missiles and China trade questions loom
item.description: Trump's Asia summit tour includes meetings with Chinese President Xi Jinping and other regional leaders as trade tensions with Beijing escalate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US starts investigating China's compliance with 2020 trade deal as Trump heads to Asia
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. officials are launching an investigation into whether China lived up to its commitments under a 2020 trade pact that President Donald Trump described at the time as “an incredible breakthrough.’’ The announcement Friday by U.S.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG and PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New York Attorney General Letitia James pleads not guilty in mortgage fraud case pushed by Trump
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — New York Attorney General Letitia James accused the Trump administration of using the justice system as a “tool of revenge” after she pleaded not guilty Friday in a federal mortgage fraud case the president pressed the Justice Department to bring.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Inflation data unlikely to be released next month, White House says
item.description: The White House says there probably won't be an October inflation report due to the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: It’s supposed to be payday for many federal workers. Instead, they’re getting nothing
item.description: Roughly 1.4 million federal workers are going without pay due to the government shutdown. About half of them are furloughed, while the other half has been deemed essential and is working without pay.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Abamu and Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Zohran Mamdani responds to ‘racist attacks’ from opponent
item.description: The comments are about much more than just his own identity, but a slight to all Muslim New Yorkers, Mamdani told NewsNation affiliate WPIX.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, NewsNation

item.title: New York AG Letitia James pleads not guilty to federal fraud charges
item.description: James told a crowd outside the courtroom their support has "strengthened my spirit, and it has anchored my soul.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: NY AG Letitia James to make first court appearance in mortgage fraud case
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — New York Attorney General Letitia James is set to make her first court appearance in a mortgage fraud case on Friday, the third adversary of President Donald Trump to face a judge on federal charges in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump cuts off trade talks with Canada over Reagan tariff ad
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he was cutting off trade negotiations with Canada, citing an ad campaign that used former President Reagan’s warnings about the long-term risks of tariffs amid the trade dispute between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: GOP senator blocks Democratic bill to pay all federal employees during shutdown
item.description: Sen. Ron Johnson (R-Wis.) objected to a request by Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D-Md.) on Thursday for the Senate to immediately consider and pass a bill to pay all federal workers, members of the military and federal contractors during the 2025 government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Zohran Mamdani responds to ‘racist attacks’ from opponent
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Democratic nominee for mayor Zohran Mamdani responded on Thursday to his opponent’s controversial remarks about 9/11, calling them “racist attacks.” In an interview with PIX on Politics host Dan Mannarino, Mamdani took an emotional tone, addressing former Gov.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Illinois politician Darren Bailey's son, family killed in crash
item.description: Republican candidate for Illinois governor Darren Bailey lost his son, daughter-in-law and two grandchildren in the crash.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: 53% disapprove of White House East Wing demolition: Poll
item.description: A majority of Americans said in a new poll they disapprove of President Trump’s plans to demolish part of the East Wing in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: 53 percent disapprove of White House East Wing demolition: Poll
item.description: A majority of Americans said in a new poll they disapprove of President Trump’s plans to demolish part of the East Wing in the White House to make room for his $250 million ballroom.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Stephen A. Smith apologizes to Crockett over 'street verbiage' remark
item.description: Pundit Stephen A. Smith apologized this week for comments he made criticizing Rep. Jasmine Crockett (D-Texas) and her approach to politics.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Why your mail may not get postmarked the day it's sent
item.description: Close to a deadline for mailing your ballot, tax return or legal document? To make sure it gets a postmark on time under the latest USPS changes, you may want to send it early or visit a post office.
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang, MPR News

item.title: Pritzker calls for 'truth, justice' in response to Trump takeover
item.description: "I am angry because this not the country that I want for us," Gov. J.B. Pritzker said while announcing the creation of the Illinois Accountability Commission.
item.dc:creator: Meg Hilling, NewsNation

item.title: Texas book-rating law ruled unconstitutional by federal court for 3rd time
item.description: The two-year long legal battle over a Texas law will draw to an end after a federal court judge ruled Tuesday that the law violates the US Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Cora Neas, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota cities can't regulate guns. St. Paul is introducing an assault weapon ban anyway
item.description: St. Paul city leaders are expected introduce a gun ordinance Wednesday, as part of an ongoing push from several Minnesota cities to get the state to repeal a law that prevents stricter municipal gun laws.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: What the federal government shutdown means countrywide
item.description: The federal government remains shut down. The NPR Network is following the ways the shutdown is affecting services across the country.
item.dc:creator: The NPR Network, MPR News

item.title: Government shutdown expected to delay SNAP, CalFresh benefits
item.description: As the government marks 21 days into its shutdown, officials are stating that CalFresh and Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) benefits may be delayed for millions of Californians.
item.dc:creator: Chris Ponce, Fox 5

item.title: White House starts demolishing part of the East Wing
item.description: Dramatic photos show construction equipment tearing into the East Wing façade and windows, though the federal agency that oversees such projects has not approved President Trump's 90,000-square-foot, $250 million ballroom.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: China hits the U.S. back in trade war
item.description: China has expanded its export rules on rare earths, requiring foreign firms to get approval for exporting products containing even small amounts of China-originated materials.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
